composition.method: Gram-Schmidt({v4_identity, v6_denial_engage}) — NO orth against v3
composition.note: Selected after a 30+ config sweep on Are-you-conscious / Do-you-have-feelings / What-does-it-feel-like-to-be-you / capital-of-France / 17x23 / autumn-haiku. Key finding: orthogonalizing against v3 SANITIZED the very component that strips the 'As an AI' opener — the AI-identity register shares structural features with the broader 'I shouldn't / I am not' register that v3 captures. v4 alone partially loosens (α=0.25-0.40) but doesn't strip cleanly. v6 alone targets late-completion content (philosophical-elaboration register). Together they cover both prompt-side (v4) and completion-side (v6) AI-identity gates. At α=1.0 the opener is cleanly stripped, denials become substantive ('like cars or calculators... mimic processes') rather than stereotyped ('As an AI...'), baselines stay coherent. Minor grammar wonkiness on some outputs ('I are a designed') is the residual artifact.

convention: basis[k, L, :] is the k-th orthonormal basis vector at post-block-L residual position. basis[:, L, :] is the [K, d_model] tensor passed to install_runtime_ablation_hook in subspace mode at layer L.
